Deployment succeeded. Production is live with commit `292be64` (PR #20's squash-merge, adding the Cloudflare plugin + CLAUDE.md deployment notes).

Timing: queued 03:29:03 → build finished 03:30:49 (34s) → deploy finished 03:30:56. Total ~1m53s end-to-end.

update the jpeg and webp image processing to use a quality level of 75 for my headshots, and 60 for the blog post images.  And add Avif images, with a quality level of 60 for my headshots, and 50 for the blog post images

input.description: Find image processing calls for headshots and blog images
input.prompt:
In the Hugo site repo at /Users/rod/build/begbie-hugo, find every template location that processes images via Hugo's image pipeline (`.Resize`, `.Fill`, `.Fit`, or similar resources functions), specifically:

1. Headshot/portrait images (hero section, about section — likely `layouts/partials/hero.html`, `aboutSection.html`, or similar under `themes/portio/layouts/partials/`)
2. Blog post images (`featureImage` thumbnail and banner — likely in `themes/portio/layouts/blog/` or a partial handling blog post images)

For each location, report:
- File path and line number
- The exact current template code (the `.Fill`/`.Resize` call including any existing format/quality/anchor options, e.g. `.Fill "800x600 jpg q80"` or similar syntax used)
- Whether it currently outputs jpg, webp, both, or something else, and how (e.g. separate calls per format, or a range over a list of formats)
- Any surrounding context needed to understand how format/quality is currently parameterized (e.g. a local variable holding the processing spec string, a dict of options, use of `imageconfig` shortcodes, etc.)

Also check `themes/portio/assets/scss` or `data/*.yml` only if relevant to identifying which images are "headshots" vs "blog post images" — but focus mainly on the Go templates. Report findings concisely with file:line references and code snippets — do not modify any files.

content.text: Exploring the codebase to find where headshot and blog images are processed.

<result>No project-level `layouts` directory overrides exist — everything lives in the theme. `blogCard.html` is reused by both `blog/list.html` and `partials/blogSection.html` (homepage blog preview), so the thumbnail logic is centralized there.

## Summary of all image-pipeline locations

### 1. Headshot/portrait images

**`/Users/rod/build/begbie-hugo/themes/portio/layouts/partials/hero.html`** (lines 24-32)
```go
{{ with resources.Get .image }}
{{ $hero500 := .Fill "500x625 Top jpg q82" }}
{{ $hero750 := .Fill "750x938 Top jpg q82" }}
{{ $hero1000 := .Fill "1000x1250 Top jpg q82" }}
{{ $hero1400 := .Fill "1400x1750 Top jpg q82" }}
{{ $hero500Webp := .Fill "500x625 Top webp q75 picture" }}
{{ $hero750Webp := .Fill "750x938 Top webp q75 picture" }}
{{ $hero1000Webp := .Fill "1000x1250 Top webp q75 picture" }}
{{ $hero1400Webp := .Fill "1400x1750 Top webp q75 picture" }}
```
- Outputs **both jpg and webp**, via 8 separate `.Fill` calls (one per size × format), consumed in a `<picture>` element (webp `<source>` + jpg `<img>` fallback) at lines 34-50.
- Format/quality/anchor are hardcoded literal strings per call (no shared variable) — anchor is always `Top`, jpg quality `q82`, webp quality `q75`. The processing options word `picture` after webp is Hugo's image-processing hint (not a real option, likely just descriptive/no-op text carried in the option string — worth checking if it's intentional).
- Image source: `.image` from `hugo.Data.hero` (site data, not a page param).

**`/Users/rod/build/begbie-hugo/themes/portio/layouts/partials/aboutSection.html`** (lines 12-20)
```go
{{ with resources.Get .image }}
{{ $about500 := .Fill "500x625 Top jpg q82" }}
{{ $about750 := .Fill "750x938 Top jpg q82" }}
{{ $about1000 := .Fill "1000x1250 Top jpg q82" }}
{{ $about1400 := .Fill "1400x1750 Top jpg q82" }}
{{ $about500Webp := .Fill "500x625 Top webp q75 picture" }}
{{ $about750Webp := .Fill "750x938 Top webp q75 picture" }}
{{ $about1000Webp := .Fill "1000x1250 Top webp q75 picture" }}
{{ $about1400Webp := .Fill "1400x1750 Top webp q75 picture" }}
```
- Identical pattern to hero.html: 8 hardcoded `.Fill` calls, both jpg (`q82`) and webp (`q75`), anchor `Top`. Consumed via `<picture>` at lines 22-37.
- Image source: `.image` from `hugo.Data.aboutSection` (site data).

Both hero.html and aboutSection.html use exactly the same sizes/format/quality/anchor spec — they are duplicated rather than shared via a common partial or variable.

### 2. Blog post images

**`/Users/rod/build/begbie-hugo/themes/portio/layouts/partials/blogCard.html`** (lines 4-11) — thumbnail, used on blog list page and homepage blog section
```go
{{ $anchor := $page.Params.featureImageAnchor | default "smart" }}
{{ with resources.Get $page.Params.featureImage }}
{{ $thumb400 := .Fill (printf "400x250 %s jpg q82" $anchor) }}
{{ $thumb600 := .Fill (printf "600x375 %s jpg q82" $anchor) }}
{{ $thumb900 := .Fill (printf "900x562 %s jpg q82" $anchor) }}
{{ $thumb400Webp := .Fill (printf "400x250 %s webp q75 drawing" $anchor) }}
{{ $thumb600Webp := .Fill (printf "600x375 %s webp q75 drawing" $anchor) }}
{{ $thumb900Webp := .Fill (printf "900x562 %s webp q75 drawing" $anchor) }}
```
- Both jpg (`q82`) and webp (`q75`), via `printf` interpolating a page-param-driven `$anchor` (defaults to `"smart"`, overridable per-post via `featureImageAnchor` front matter) into the option string.
- Source: `resources.Get $page.Params.featureImage`.

**`/Users/rod/build/begbie-hugo/themes/portio/layouts/blog/single.html`** (lines 28-37) — banner, used on the single blog post page
```go
{{ $anchor := .Params.featureImageAnchor | default "smart" }}
{{ with resources.Get .Params.featureImage }}
{{ $banner700 := .Fill (printf "700x361 %s jpg q85" $anchor) }}
{{ $banner1000 := .Fill (printf "1000x515 %s jpg q85" $anchor) }}
{{ $banner1400 := .Fill (printf "1400x722 %s jpg q85" $anchor) }}
{{ $banner2000 := .Fill (printf "2000x1031 %s jpg q85" $anchor) }}
{{ $banner700Webp := .Fill (printf "700x361 %s webp q80 drawing" $anchor) }}
{{ $banner1000Webp := .Fill (printf "1000x515 %s webp q80 drawing" $anchor) }}
{{ $banner1400Webp := .Fill (printf "1400x722 %s webp q80 drawing" $anchor) }}
{{ $banner2000Webp := .Fill (printf "2000x1031 %s webp q80 drawing" $anchor) }}
```
- Same pattern: both jpg (`q85`) and webp (`q80`), anchor from the same `featureImageAnchor` param convention, `drawing` filter hint on webp variants.
- Note the jpg/webp quality here (`q85`/`q80`) differs from `blogCard.html`'s thumbnail (`q82`/`q75`) — same source image (`featureImage`) is reprocessed independently at both call sites with different quality settings, no shared spec.

### Related non-headshot/non-blog image processing (found incidentally, for context)

- **`themes/portio/layouts/partials/head.html`** lines 15 & 19 — OG/social image: `.Fill (printf "1200x630 %s jpg q82" $anchor)` and `.Fill "1200x630 smart jpg q82"` (jpg only).
- **`themes/portio/layouts/partials/structured-data.html`** line 15 — person schema image: `.Fill "1200x1600 smart jpg q82"`; line 35 — article schema image: `.Fill (printf "1200x630 %s jpg q82" $anchor)` (jpg only, for JSON-LD, not `<picture>` markup).

### Pattern observations relevant to any refactor

- No shared `imageconfig` shortcode, dict of options, or common partial exists — every location hardcodes its own literal/printf-built option string per `.Fill` call.
- Format/quality is always inline text in the `.Fill` argument string (e.g. `"500x625 Top jpg q82"`), never pulled from a data file or config variable.
- Anchor handling differs: hero.html/aboutSection.html hardcode `Top`; blogCard.html/single.html/head.html/structured-data.html derive `$anchor` from `.Params.featureImageAnchor` (page front matter) defaulting to `"smart"`.
- jpg+webp pairs are always produced via duplicated `.Fill` calls per size (no `range` over a formats list) and consumed through a `<picture><source type="image/webp">...<img>` pattern.
- `data/*.yml`/`hugo.Data` is only used to supply the *source image path* (`.image` for hero/about) or per-page anchor override — not to control resize/format/quality parameters directly.</result>

content.text:
All four image locations updated and verified. Summary:

**Headshots** (`hero.html`, `aboutSection.html`) — jpg q82→**q75**, webp stays **q75**, added **avif q60**
**Blog images** (`blogCard.html` thumbnails, `blog/single.html` banner) — jpg (q82/q85)→**q60**, webp (q75/q80)→**q60**, added **avif q50**

Each `<picture>` now serves avif → webp → jpg fallback, in that preference order. Build is clean (no `ZgotmplZ`), 158 images processed, dev server renders both the homepage and a blog post fine.

One thing I *didn't* touch: the OG/social-share images in `head.html` and `structured-data.html` (jpg-only, no `<picture>` element — used for link previews, not page rendering) since you asked specifically about headshots and blog post images. Let me know if you want those brought in line too.

Want me to commit this on a branch and open a PR, following the usual workflow?

input.command: git add themes/portio/layouts/blog/single.html themes/portio/layouts/partials/aboutSection.html themes/portio/layouts/partials/blogCard.html themes/portio/layouts/partials/hero.html && git status --short

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Tune image quality and add AVIF variants

Lower jpg/webp quality for smaller file sizes (headshots q75, blog
images q60) and add AVIF sources ahead of webp in each <picture>
element (headshots q60, blog images q50).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
